title: Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib
tagline: Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib is a Sikh gurdwara or place of worship in the city of Fatehgarh Sahib in the Indian state of Punjab.
overview.description: Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib is a Sikh gurdwara or place of worship in the city of Fatehgarh Sahib in the Indian state of Punjab. The gurdwara marks the 1710 conquest of the city by the Sikhs under the leadership of Banda Singh Bahadur. Sikhs captured the area and razed the fort built by Ferozshah Tughlaq to the ground.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sirhind
topPlaces.description: Sirhind is a twin city of Fatehgarh Sahib in Punjab, India. It is hosts the municipal council of Fatehgarh Sahib district.

topPlaces.name: Aam Khas Bagh
topPlaces.description: Aam Khas Bagh is the remains of a highway-inn. It was divided into two parts - the Aam for public use and the Khas for private use by royalty.

topPlaces.name: Sadna Qasai Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Sadna Qasai Mosque is a former mosque in a ruinous state, located in Sirhind, in the state of Punjab, India. It is listed as a state protected monument.

topPlaces.name: Fatehgarh Sahib
topPlaces.description: Fatehgarh Sahib (Punjabi pronunciation: [fə.t̪éː.gə́ɽᵊ sä́ːbᵊ]) is a city and a sacred pilgrimage site of Sikhism in the north west Indian state of Punjab. It is the headquarters of Fatehgarh Sahib district, located about 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) north of Sirhind.

topPlaces.name: Bhatt Majra, Fatehgarh Sahib
topPlaces.description: Bhatt Majra is a village in Sirhind mandal, district Fatehgarh Sahib, state Punjab, India. The village is on the world map for organic farming, visited by Prince Charles in 2005.

topPlaces.name: Khanda Museum
topPlaces.description: Khanda Museum is a museum located at Fatehgarh Sahib and its building constructed in shape of Sikh religious symbol Khanda. The museum is built by SGPC in the memory of Sikh-warrior Banda Singh Bahadur and would exhibit his history.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Manupur
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Manupur took place on 11 March 1748 between the forces of the rising Durrani Empire led by Afghan Emperor Ahmad Shah Durrani and the armies of the Mughal Empire led by Mughal Vizier Qamar ud-Din. It was part of Ahmad Shah's first invasion of India.

topPlaces.name: Mandi Mullanpur
topPlaces.description: Mandi Mullanpur, also known as Mullanpur, is a town located 18 km from Ludhiana on Grand Trunk Road to Jagraon. The village of Mullanpur is connected to the town of Mandi-Mullanpur via two link roads.

items.note: Sirhind is a twin city of Fatehgarh Sahib in Punjab, India. It is hosts the municipal council of Fatehgarh Sahib district.
Aam Khas Bagh is the remains of a highway-inn. It was divided into two parts - the Aam for public use and the Khas for private use by royalty.
The Sadna Qasai Mosque is a former mosque in a ruinous state, located in Sirhind, in the state of Punjab, India. It is listed as a state protected monument.

Fatehgarh Sahib (Punjabi pronunciation: [fə.t̪éː.gə́ɽᵊ sä́ːbᵊ]) is a city and a sacred pilgrimage site of Sikhism in the north west Indian state of Punjab. It is the headquarters of Fatehgarh Sahib district, located about 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) north of Sirhind.
Bhatt Majra is a village in Sirhind mandal, district Fatehgarh Sahib, state Punjab, India. The village is on the world map for organic farming, visited by Prince Charles in 2005.

Khanda Museum is a museum located at Fatehgarh Sahib and its building constructed in shape of Sikh religious symbol Khanda. The museum is built by SGPC in the memory of Sikh-warrior Banda Singh Bahadur and would exhibit his history.

The Battle of Manupur took place on 11 March 1748 between the forces of the rising Durrani Empire led by Afghan Emperor Ahmad Shah Durrani and the armies of the Mughal Empire led by Mughal Vizier Qamar ud-Din. It was part of Ahmad Shah's first invasion of India.

Mandi Mullanpur, also known as Mullanpur, is a town located 18 km from Ludhiana on Grand Trunk Road to Jagraon. The village of Mullanpur is connected to the town of Mandi-Mullanpur via two link roads.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib?
faq.a: The best time to visit Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (39 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (41 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib?
faq.a: Stays in Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Gurdwara Fatehgarh Sahib?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sirhind, Aam Khas Bagh, Sadna Qasai Mosque, Fatehgarh Sahib, Bhatt Majra, Fatehgarh Sahib.
